mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary accommodation, compressing discount rates and supporting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS level on 20-day returns is less than 0.05 over any 2-year window, or forward returns are negative in >60% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, it indicates severe labor market slack that depresses aggregate demand and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as fear of prolonged recession intensifies.
failure_condition: If triggered instances yield positive 5-day returns more than 50% of the time, or if the average return conditioned on trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals a demand-supply collapse that overshoots central bank reaction, and when both contract month-over-month, equity markets anticipate a policy response and rally over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline > 0 and INDPRO month-over-month decline > 0
failure_condition: If joint contraction instances produce negative mean 20-day return, or if the interaction term in a linear regression of returns on RSAFS and INDPRO changes is not significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: In a deep recession with near-zero interest rates, rising unemployment signals additional fiscal or monetary stimulus will be deployed, boosting equities over the next 60 trading days; in normal conditions, rising unemployment is negative.
failure_condition: If in the regime where UNRATE>7% and FEDFUNDS<1%, the correlation between UNRATE changes and 60-day returns is negative, or if in normal regimes it is positive (contradicting flip).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) is a leading indicator of economic activity; a decline in INDPRO signals weakening supply that will reduce earnings and lead to negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the monthly change in INDPRO has a rank IC of less than 0.03 with 20-day forward returns, or if positive month-on-month changes produce more than 50% negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) decline by more than 1% month-over-month, it signals collapse in consumer spending that will continue to pressure equities negatively over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If triggered instances yield positive 5-day returns more than 40% of the time, or average return is not negative at 10% significance.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) is declining month-over-month, deflation risk emerges, devastating corporate profits through falling prices and wages, leading to negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days especially with rates at zero.
failure_condition: If declining CPI months produce positive average 20-day returns, or if rank IC of CPI change is positive over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When both nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) decline simultaneously month-over-month, it confirms persistent economic weakness that delays recovery, leading to negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0 and RSAFS month-over-month change < 0
failure_condition: If in instances where both decline, the mean 20-day return is positive, or if the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p<0.10).
